title: ortholoma lehmannii
intro: Ortholoma lehmannii, also known as Wild Celery or American Wild Celery, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the Apiaceae family. Its medicinal properties lie in its roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and inflammation. The plant has been found to possess health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing coughs, and alleviating menstrual cramps. The roots can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried for use in medicinal blends, making it a valuable resource in traditional herbal medicine.
